I audited Turn 38‚Äôs live thread and found one important correction plus one refinement of the mixed (e,f)=(3,2) obstruction target.The only still-unrefuted candidate remains the dynamic tame-inertia word recorded in the prior log:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,ŌÄ=ŌÄ2,ő∑=2ŌÄ,with ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).This is in the format requested by the prompt: ŌÉ,ŌĄ are the distinguished tame generators, the relation ŌĄŌÉ=ŌĄ2 is implicit, and x,y are pro-2-core generators.The earlier proposals are not live: the prior file records concrete failures for the scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants.The external structural benchmark is also unchanged. Neukirch‚Äôs report on Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg says the proof method for the known pÓÄ†=2 presentations studies, for each finite normal tame subextension K/k, the DemuŇ°kin group Gal(K(p)/K), the finite tame action, and then uses a Koch/DemuŇ°kin-formation uniqueness theorem; this is stronger than merely checking ranks or abstract DemuŇ°kin kernels.Numdam Jarden‚ÄďShusterman explicitly note that the Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg explicit description is for pÓÄ†=2, that Diekert‚Äôs dyadic analogue does not cover K=Q2, and that local absolute Galois groups have minimal generator number [K:Qp]+2.Mathematics at TAU The first correction: Turn 38 still had the sign-cover geometric series backwards. Let V=kerőļ be the sign double cover, let t be its deck involution, and work on a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j=1‚ąítőĪa 1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít.

The reason is that ŌÄ‚Č°1(mod2) and ŌÄ‚Č°0(mode), so (tőĪa)ŌÄ=t. This is the only direction compatible with the unit/radical argument: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit for őĪaÓÄ†=1, while 1‚ąít lies in the radical. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient of the correcting factorx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. Thus the prior conclusion survives‚ÄĒnontrivial odd tame-inertia blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots‚ÄĒbut Turn 38‚Äôs displayed fraction must be inverted.The second correction is the trivial tame-inertia elimination. With T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

If f=2a m with m odd, then s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). For even f, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. The valid substitution is therefore

Y=P(T)X+YňČ,P(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,up to the harmless convention T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. With h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T‚ąí1),one gets, for a nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), hence gcd(f,2s+1)=1. So h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and the trivial block remains nonsingular. Again, the prior conclusion survives, but the quotient direction matters.Using the corrected formulas, the finite-tame abelian and sign-kernel checks remain:Ue,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and, for the sign kernel Ve,f=kerőļ,Ve,fab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4.

The C2 and C4 torsion is entirely in the tame-inertia-invariant block; nontrivial odd tame-character blocks are unit-pivot blocks.The new refinement beyond Turn 38 concerns the first mixed even-f filtered-action class for (e,f)=(3,2). Turn 38 computed only the b-component:őĪ(b)b‚ąí1‚Č°Z0=i‚ąąZ/3‚ąĎ[Ai,Bi](modU3).That is useful, but the actual first-order operator class must preserve the quadratic DemuŇ°kin initial relationq=i‚ąąZ/3‚ąĎ(Ai[2]+Bi[2])+[b,S].

Because the regular F2[S3]-part contributes no cohomology after taking the C3 -fixed, C2 -operator quotient, the b-component Z0 determines the cohomology class modulo relation-preserving boundaries. A relation-preserving representative is:őī0 (b)=Z0,őī0(Ai)=[Bi,S],őī0(Bi)=[Ai,S],őī0 (S)=0.

Indeed,őī0(q)=i‚ąĎ([Ai,[Bi,S]]+[Bi,[Ai,S]])+[Z0,S]=[Z0,S]+[Z0,S]=0 in characteristic 2, using Jacobi. So the immediate arithmetic target is sharper than Turn 38 stated: one should not merely check whether arithmetic gives őĪ(b)b‚ąí1=Z0; one should check whether the arithmetic S3 -operator class equals the full relation-preserving class őī0.

In Turn 37‚Äôs decompositionN=Z0+Z1+Z2,M1=Z0+Z1,M2=Z0+Z2,the equality Z0=N+M1+M2 means the candidate selects both mixed augmentation-zero directions nontrivially. The next arithmetic computation must therefore detect not just the unramified-looking line N, but also the two genuinely mixed tame components M1,M2.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I corrected two formula directions:Da=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít,P(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís (up to T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1). With these corrections, the prior abelianization and sign-kernel conclusions still hold. I also sharpened Turn 38‚Äôs (3,2) mixed-Frobenius target: the candidate‚Äôs first mixed class is represented by the full relation-preserving derivationőī0 (b)=Z0,őī0(Ai)=[Bi,S],őī0(Bi)=[Ai,S],őī0 (S)=0.

GAP: This is still not a complete presentation proof. The arithmetic side must be shown to realize the same relation-preserving mixed class őī0 over the tame S3 -extension L=Q2(ő∂3,21/3), and then the comparison must be extended to the full filtered even-Frobenius action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Compute the arithmetic S3 -operator action on GL (2)/GL(2)3 or GL (2)/GL(2)5 via explicit Kummer/Witt data, with the immediate target of verifying the full őī0 class, including the two nonzero mixed augmentation-zero components.[BREAKTHROUGH]
